0101.title: COMPLETE TRAVELER INFORMATION LIST REPORT

quotes.text: Retrieve and reconcile reports, listed as LDTA required in Chapter 6, from the DTS website and ensure they are maintained for the current plus the previous year.
LDTA Report. Complete Traveler Information List Report. This Report shall be retrieved monthly by the LDTA to verify appropriate permission levels and separation of duties.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must retrieve the Complete Traveler Information List Report monthly from DTS, reconcile permission levels and separation of duties, and retain the report for the current plus previous calendar year.

howToInspect.step: Request The Last 12+ Months Of CTIL Reports
actions: Pull monthly reports for the inspection period.
Confirm reports are dated for each month with no gaps.
Cross-check permission levels in the report against DD Form 577 appointments.
Identify any permission level not supported by a current DD Form 577.
Confirm no individual holds incompatible role combinations per Ch. 2 Table 2-1.
Confirm reports retained for current plus previous calendar year.

questionsToAsk: When did you last pull the Complete Traveler Information List Report?
How do you reconcile permissions against DD Form 577?
Where are the previous year's reports retained?
verification: CTIL Report retrieved monthly for the inspection period.
Separation of duties verified per Ch. 2 Table 2-1.
Reports retained for current plus previous calendar year.

0102.title: ALL REQUIRED ROLES APPOINTED VIA DD FORM 577

quotes.text: Ensure Organizational Defense Travel Administrators (ODTAs) are properly appointed by commanders or their designated representatives, within the geographic area, using DD Form 577.

Retain (electronically or hardcopy) copies of all DD Form 577s for appointed positions within the geographic area in the LDTA office, with the exception of Disbursing Office/Finance Office (DO/FO) appointments or outside the LDTAs geographic area of responsibility.

regulatory.summary: All DTS roles (LDTAs, ODTAs, FDTAs, BDTAs, AOs, NDEAs, ROs, DMMs) must be appointed via DD Form 577. The LDTA retains copies of DD Form 577 for all appointed positions within the geographic area in the LDTA office.

actions: Request the complete DTS role roster from the LDTA office.
Cross-check against the command's DTS organization tree.

howToInspect.step: Pull DD Form 577 For Each Role
actions: Request the file of DD Form 577 appointments retained by the LDTA.
Confirm each DD Form 577 is signed by the commander or designated representative.
Confirm forms are not expired (10-year retention from appointment).

howToInspect.step: Verify AO DD Form 577 Submission To DFAS
actions: Confirm AO appointment letters were forwarded to DFAS Indianapolis (JFD) at dfas-incddd577@dfas.mil.
Role active in DTS without DD Form 577 on file.
AO appointments not forwarded to DFAS.

questionsToAsk: Where do you retain DD Form 577 appointments?
How do you confirm AO appointments were received by DFAS?
When did you last reconcile the DTS role roster against DD Form 577 file?
verification: DD Form 577 on file for every appointed DTS role.
AO appointments forwarded to DFAS Indianapolis.
Forms not expired and signed by appropriate appointing authority.
Role roster reconciles to DD Form 577 file.

0103.title: AO DD FORM 577 SUBMITTED TO DFAS VIA E-MAIL

quotes.text: Collect and submit all Authorizing Official (certifying officials) appointment letters to the central electronic repository at the DFAS Indianapolis Disbursing Operations Directorate (JFD), 8899 East 56th Street, Indianapolis, IN 46249 (secure e-mail dfas-incddd577@dfas.mil). The original of the DD Form 577 requiring a wet signature must be mailed to the above address. However, digitally signed DD Form 577s may be submitted electronically. DD Form 577s may be viewed on the Electronic Document Access (EDA) website.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must submit all AO (certifying officials) DD Form 577 appointment letters to the DFAS Indianapolis Disbursing Operations Directorate (JFD) via secure e-mail dfas-incddd577@dfas.mil. Wet-signed originals must be mailed to JFD. Digitally signed forms may be submitted electronically.

howToInspect.step: Identify AO Appointments In The Inspection Period
actions: Pull list of AOs appointed in the past 24 months.

howToInspect.step: Verify DFAS Submission For Each AO
actions: Request the LDTA's submission record to dfas-incddd577@dfas.mil.
Confirm each AO's DD Form 577 was submitted.

howToInspect.step: Verify Method (Mail Wet Sig / E-mail Digital Sig)
actions: For wet-signed originals, confirm mailed to JFD Indianapolis address.
For digital signatures, confirm electronic submission.
Confirm AO DD Form 577s appear in EDA where access is established.
AO DD Form 577 not submitted to DFAS.
Wet-signed form submitted only electronically without mailing original.

questionsToAsk: How do you submit AO DD Form 577s to DFAS?
Show me the submission record for AOs appointed in the last 24 months?
verification: Submission record to DFAS dfas-incddd577@dfas.mil on file.
Wet-signed originals mailed to JFD Indianapolis when applicable.
Digitally signed forms submitted electronically when applicable.
AO appointments confirmed via EDA access where available.

0104.title: STANDARD NAVAL CORRESPONDENCE WAIVER APPROVAL LETTERS

quotes.text: Waivers. Waivers to specific grade or duty assignment requirements established in this Order shall be submitted via standard naval correspondence and approved by the first O-6 or GS-15 in the supervisory chain. Only the following may be waived: (1) AOs in a grade below those listed in this Order; (2) LDTAs/ODTAs serving in routing lists. Waivers shall be on a by-name basis.

regulatory.summary: Waivers to grade or duty requirements (AO below required grade; LDTA/ODTA in routing lists) require by-name standard naval correspondence approval by the first O-6 or GS-15 in the supervisory chain. No other waivers are authorized.

actions: Review AO appointments for grade compliance.
Review routing lists for LDTA/ODTA assignments.
Request standard naval correspondence waiver letters for each identified case.
Confirm letters signed by the first O-6 or GS-15 in the supervisory chain.
Reject any waiver signed below the required level.
Confirm each waiver is by-name for a specific individual.
No waiver for AO below required grade.

questionsToAsk: Which AOs in your command are below the required grade?
Are any LDTAs or ODTAs included in routing lists?
Show me the by-name waiver letters?
verification: Waiver letters on file for each below-grade AO.
Waiver letters on file for each LDTA/ODTA in routing lists.
Letters signed by first O-6 or GS-15 in supervisory chain.
Each waiver is by-name and on standard naval correspondence.

0105.title: LDTA AND AO CERTIFYING OFFICER LEGISLATION (COL) TRAINING

quotes.text: Ensure personnel requiring Certifying Officer Legislation (COL) certification have completed the training as listed in Chapter 5.
Certifying Officer Legislation training must be completed prior to appointment and annually thereafter for personnel performing AO duties involving certification of payment.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must ensure each AO has completed COL initial training prior to appointment and the COL refresher annually thereafter. LDTAs must also complete COL training if they perform certifying duties.

howToInspect.step: Pull AO And LDTA Training Records
actions: Request COL initial and refresher training certificates for each AO and LDTA.
For each AO, confirm initial COL certificate date predates the DD Form 577 appointment date.
Confirm refresher completed within 12 months of the most recent prior certificate.
Identify any AO with refresher overdue.
Confirm certificates retained for current plus previous calendar year.
AO appointed without prior COL training.

questionsToAsk: Show me the COL certificates for each AO?
How do you track refresher training due dates?
verification: Initial COL certificate predates each AO appointment.
Annual refresher completed within 12 months for each AO.
Certificates on file for current plus previous calendar year.
LDTA COL training completed where performing certifying duties.

0106.title: APPOINTEE TRAINING WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF APPOINTMENT

quotes.text: Conduct ODTA, FDTA, AO, NDEA, SAO, RO, DMM, and Traveler initial and annual refresher training as listed in Chapter 5. Maintain a copy of attendance rosters for the current plus the previous year.

Assign, manage, and monitor permission levels to ensure appropriate separation of duties in accordance with Chapter 2. Appropriate training shall be completed before DTS permissions are assigned (see Chapter 5 for training requirements). DTS permissions shall be revoked immediately should a lapse in training occur.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must conduct initial training and ensure appointees complete required training within two weeks of appointment. DTS permissions must not be assigned until training is complete. Permissions must be revoked immediately on training lapse. Attendance rosters retained for current plus previous calendar year.

howToInspect.step: Identify All Appointees In Inspection Period
actions: Pull list of appointments via DD Form 577 in past 24 months.
For each appointee, compute days from appointment to training completion.

howToInspect.step: Verify Permissions Not Assigned Before Training
actions: Spot-check CTIL Report timing against training completion.
Identify any appointee with training lapse.
Confirm rosters retained for current plus previous calendar year.
Training lapsed but permissions not revoked.

questionsToAsk: How do you track training completion against the two-week window?
What is your process when training lapses?
verification: Training within two weeks for each appointee in the inspection period.
Permissions not assigned before training complete.
Permissions revoked immediately on training lapse.
Attendance rosters retained for current plus previous calendar year.

0107.title: DMO REVIEWERS INSERTED INTO AUTHORIZATION ROUTING LISTS

quotes.text: Insert the Distribution Management Office (DMO) into all authorization routing lists when the organization is serviced by a Marine Corps DMO. This does not include routing lists for vouchers. When adding the DMO Reviewer to the routing list ensure the bypass Passenger Name Record (PNR) process name is selected.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must insert the DMO into all authorization routing lists when serviced by a Marine Corps DMO (excluding voucher routing lists). The DMO Reviewer must be added with the bypass PNR process name selected.

howToInspect.step: Determine If Command Is Serviced By A Marine Corps DMO

actions: Request routing lists for all authorizations.
Confirm DMO Reviewer present in each authorization routing list.
Confirm bypass PNR process name applied to DMO Reviewer entry.

howToInspect.step: Verify DMO Not In Voucher Routing
actions: Confirm DMO not present in voucher routing lists.
DMO incorrectly placed in voucher routing.

questionsToAsk: Is your command serviced by a Marine Corps DMO?
Show me an authorization routing list and the DMO entry?
verification: DMO Reviewer present in all authorization routing lists.
Bypass PNR process selected on each DMO entry.
DMO not present in voucher routing lists.

0108.title: READ ONLY ACCESS (ROA) REQUESTS AND REVALIDATION

quotes.text: Facilitate Read Only Access (ROA) requests for individuals in their geographic area. ROA requests shall be submitted to the Marine Corps DTS Help Desk at USMCDTSHELPDESK@URS.COM for approval.

regulatory.summary: The LDTA must facilitate ROA requests for individuals in their geographic area, submit to the Marine Corps DTS Help Desk for approval, and revalidate access once the form expires.

actions: Request the list of current ROA users in the LDTA's geographic area.
Pull the submission record for each ROA request.

howToInspect.step: Verify Submission To Marine Corps DTS Help Desk

actions: Confirm revalidation submitted before access continued.

questionsToAsk: How do you track ROA expirations?
Show me the most recent ROA revalidation?
verification: ROA request records on file for each active holder.
Expired ROA forms revalidated or access removed.

0109.title: DMM REPORTS - DEBT MANAGEMENT AND APPROVED STATUS

quotes.text: DMM Report. This report shall be used to identify DTS travel debts that require action by the DMM. Refer to DMM procedures in Chapter 9 of this Order and the DTS Document Guide to Managing Travel Incurred Debt located in the DTS Reference Library. Commanders shall certify (sign) the weekly report reflecting collection actions taken for debts older than 30 days.
Approved Status Report. This report shall be reviewed for vouchers in an AR REJECT status. The DMM shall contact AOs with instructions on how to correct the rejected documents.

regulatory.summary: The DMM must reconcile the Debt Management Report weekly to identify DTS travel debts requiring action, with the commander certifying the weekly report reflecting collection actions for debts older than 30 days. The DMM must also review the Approved Status Report weekly for vouchers in AR REJECT status and contact AOs with correction instructions. Both reports retained for current plus previous calendar year.

actions: Request DMM Reports for past 12 months.

howToInspect.step: Verify Commander Certification For Debts Over 30 Days
actions: Confirm commander signature on the weekly report reflecting collection actions.

howToInspect.step: Pull Approved Status Reports With AR REJECT
actions: Pull reports showing AR REJECT status.

howToInspect.step: Verify AO Notifications For AR REJECT
actions: Confirm DMM contacted AOs with correction instructions.
Confirm reports retained current plus previous calendar year.
Commander not certifying for 30+ day debts.
AOs not contacted on AR REJECT.

questionsToAsk: Show me the DMM Report and Approved Status Report from this past week?
Show me the commander certification for debts older than 30 days?
verification: Weekly DMM Reports retained for current plus previous calendar year.
Commander signature on report for debts aged over 30 days.
AO contact documented for AR REJECT vouchers.

0110.title: FDTA APPROVED STATUS REPORT BI-WEEKLY RECONCILIATION

quotes.text: Approved Status Report. The FDTA shall retrieve and reconcile this report every two weeks (bi-weekly/bi-monthly). Reports shall be maintained for the current plus the previous year.

regulatory.summary: The FDTA must retrieve and reconcile the Approved Status Report every two weeks (bi-weekly/bi-monthly) and retain the reports for the current plus previous calendar year.

actions: Confirm the FDTA appointment via DD Form 577.
Confirm cadence is every two weeks.
Confirm each report shows action taken on AR REJECT vouchers.
Confirm reports retained current plus previous calendar year.

questionsToAsk: Show me the last six FDTA Approved Status Reports?
How do you act on AR REJECT vouchers?
verification: FDTA appointment via DD Form 577 on file.
Reconciliation annotations present on each report.
Reports retained current plus previous calendar year.

0201.title: COMPLETE TRAVELER INFORMATION LIST REPORT

regulatory.summary: ODTAs must extract the Complete Traveler Information List report monthly, conduct thorough reconciliation including verification of profile accuracy and DD Form 577 alignment, annotate all discrepancies and actions taken, and retain reports for the current plus previous calendar year. Header information must remain unaltered. Inspector Note: Reconciliation includes verifying and annotating the following: 1) information for accuracy. Profile information, SSN, address, unit information, GTCC duplication, EFT data, etc. 2) Ensuring permission levels are consistent with DD Form 577 appointments and that appropriate separation of duties are maintained.

actions: Ask: "Show me your Complete Traveler Information List reports for the current year and previous year."
Should have: Current year (all months) + previous full year
Example: If the current month is the inspection month, calculate back 24 months (15 months minimum retention)
Check: Report dated for each month
howToInspect.callout: CTIL not extracted monthly

actions: CRITICAL: System header must be unaltered
Verify: Report title, date, system-generated info intact
Look for: Deleted rows, modified headers, reformatted data
howToInspect.callout: If header altered

actions: Reports MUST be annotated to show work performed
Look for: Handwritten notes, highlights, electronic comments
If no annotations: DISCREPANCY (not being reconciled)
howToInspect.callout: No reconciliation annotation on report

actions: For each report, verify annotations show:
GTCC duplication identified (same person, multiple GTCC entries)
howToInspect.callout: Profile inaccuracy identified but not corrected

actions: Action taken to correct EFT issues
howToInspect.callout: EFT data outdated and not validated

actions: Critical requirement: Permission levels must match DD 577 appointments
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 577 alignment mismatch (permission without appointment)

actions: AO cannot also be traveler on own vouchers
ODTA cannot be AO in own organization
RO cannot be traveler on documents they route
howToInspect.callout: Separation of duties violation identified (incompatible role combination)

actions: Select 5-10 travelers from most recent report
Ask ODTA: "Show me the DD 577 for this person."
Verify: Permissions in DTS match DD 577

howToInspect.callout: If reports missing

actions: Ask: "Walk me through how you reconcile this report each month."
howToInspect.callout: Retention period not met for prior year reports

actions: Separation of duties violations not caught
Discrepancies identified but no action taken
Reports not retained for required period

questionsToAsk: Show me how you reconcile this report each month?
How do you verify permissions match DD Form 577 appointments?
Have you found any GTCC duplications? How did you resolve them?
Show me examples of discrepancies you've identified and corrected?
Do you have reports for the entire current and previous calendar year?

0202.title: DD FORM 577 APPOINTMENTS

regulatory.summary: All DTS roles must be formally appointed using the current electronic DD Form 577 with digital signatures from both the appointing official and appointee. Appointments must be maintained for 10 years. No pen changes allowed. Delegation authority and Assumption of Command documentation must be on file. Inspector Note: Appointments must be completed using the most current DD577 form version at the time of appointment. Pen changes are not authorized. DD Form 577s must be digitally signed by commander or designated representative and the appointee. Personnel with group or organization access outside their designated organization must provide justification. DD Form 577s must be retained for 10 years. Assumption of Command letters and Delegation of Authority documents must be retained on file.

actions: Ask: "Show me all current DD Form 577s for DTS roles."
Should have appointments for ALL roles present in organization
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 577 file incomplete - role active without appointment

actions: Create roster of who should have DD 577:
Check: Form version number on each DD 577
Compare: Appointment date to form version effective date
Must use: Current version at time of appointment
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 577 form version outdated

actions: Look for: Handwritten modifications, strikethroughs, added text
If pen changes present: DISCREPANCY (form must be redone)
howToInspect.callout: Pen-change correction on DD Form 577 without re-signature
Digital signature invalid or unverifiable

actions: If commander: No further verification needed
If designee: Request Delegation of Authority letter
Verify: Unbroken delegation chain from commander to signatory
howToInspect.callout: Appointing official below required authority level

actions: Request: Current commander's Assumption of Command letter
Compare: DD 577 dates to commander tenure
howToInspect.callout: Assumption of command documentation missing for new appointing authority

actions: Review each DD 577: What organizations does person have access to?
Standard: Access only to person's assigned organization
howToInspect.callout: Outside-organization access without documented approval

actions: Ask: "Do you have DD Form 577s from personnel who left in the past 10 years?"
Should retain: All DD 577s for 10 years from date of separation/transfer
Check filing system: Organized for easy retrieval?
howToInspect.callout: DD Form 577 not retained 10 years

actions: Commander or properly delegated official signed
Compare: DTS permissions to DD Form 577 appointments
howToInspect.callout: Sampled DD Form 577 does not match DTS system permission

actions: Missing DD Form 577 for current personnel
No Delegation of Authority on file
No Assumption of Command letter on file
DD Form 577s not retained for 10 years
Permissions in DTS don't match DD 577

questionsToAsk: Show me the DD Form 577s for all your current AOs?
Who signed these DD 577s - commander or designee?
If designee, show me the Delegation of Authority letter?
Show me your current commander's Assumption of Command letter?
Do you have any personnel with access outside your organization? Show me the justification?

0203.title: ROUTING LIST MAINTENANCE

regulatory.summary: ODTAs must ensure all AOs and ROs are properly inserted into authorization and voucher routing lists appropriate for their designated roles. Routing lists must be maintained monthly in conjunction with the Complete Traveler Information List review. Inspector Note: Conduct routing list maintenance during the monthly Complete Traveler Information List review.

actions: Ask: "Show me your authorization and voucher routing lists in DTS."
Should have: Routing lists for each organizational unit
howToInspect.callout: Current routing list not on file

actions: From DD Form 577 file: List all appointed AOs and ROs

howToInspect.callout: AO missing from authorization routing list
RO missing from voucher routing list

actions: Check: No routing loops or dead ends
howToInspect.callout: Routing list structure does not match required template

actions: Observe: Who appears in routing list?
Verify: Correct AO and RO present
Ask ODTA: "Show me routing for a recent voucher."
Check: Correct AO and RO in routing?
Verify: No missing or unauthorized personnel
Ask: "When did you last update routing lists?"
Should be: Monthly (during Complete Traveler Info List review)
Request proof: Notes on Complete Traveler Info List report
Check: Annotations showing routing list maintenance
howToInspect.callout: Monthly routing list maintenance not documented

actions: Any AOs or ROs appointed in last 30 days?
If yes: Verify immediately added to routing lists
howToInspect.callout: New AO/RO not added to routing within required window

actions: Ask: "Has anyone left your command in the past 3 months?"
If AO or RO separated: Verify removed from routing lists
If still in routing lists: FINDING (security/financial risk)
howToInspect.callout: Separated personnel still in routing list

actions: If LDTA or ODTA in routing list: Must have waiver (see Q0204)
howToInspect.callout: Waiver required but not on file (LDTA/ODTA in routing)

actions: Separated personnel still in routing lists
LDTA/ODTA in routing list without waiver
Routing lists don't match DD Form 577 appointments

questionsToAsk: Show me your authorization and voucher routing lists?
Walk me through the routing flow for a typical TDY authorization?
When did you last update your routing lists?
Have you had any AO or RO changes recently? Are they in the routing lists?

0204.title: WAIVER LETTERS

regulatory.summary: Two specific situations require formal waiver letters signed by the first O-6 or GS-15 in the supervisory chain: (1) Appointment of AOs in grades below SNCO or GS-7, and (2) Placement of LDTAs or ODTAs in routing lists. Waivers must be by-name, standard naval correspondence format, and retained on file. Inspector Note: Waiver(s) must be retained and available for review.

howToInspect.callout: Waiver required but no by-name letter on file

actions: Standard: SNCO (E-7) or above, or GS-7 or above
If any AO below this grade: Waiver required
howToInspect.callout: Waiver letter signed below O-6 or GS-15 supervisory chain

howToInspect.step: Waiver Type 2 - Ldta/Odta In Routing List
actions: Check: Is LDTA or ODTA in authorization/voucher routing?
Standard: Should NOT be in routing lists
If LDTA/ODTA in routing: Waiver required
Ask: "Do you have any personnel requiring waivers?"
If yes: "Show me the waiver letters."
Should have: One waiver per person per waivable item
From: First O-6 or GS-15 in chain
Must be: First O-6 or GS-15 in supervisory chain
If O-5 or below: DISCREPANCY (insufficient authority)
If GS-14 or below: DISCREPANCY (insufficient authority)
Individual identified by name and rank/grade

howToInspect.step: Verify Waiver Content - LDTA/ODTA in Routing
actions: For each LDTA/ODTA in routing list:
Individual identified by name and position
Reason LDTA/ODTA must be in routing list
AO promoted (may no longer need waiver)
If status changed: Verify waiver still valid or new waiver obtained
Waivers must be retained on file
Should be: Filed with DD Form 577 or in separate waiver file
Verify individual is actually serving as AO
Verify individual actually in routing list
If not in routing list: Waiver may not be needed
Waiver signed by insufficient authority (below O-6/GS-15)
Waiver not by-name (blanket or generic waiver)
Waiver not in standard naval correspondence format
Individual's status changed, waiver no longer valid
Waiver references wrong individual or wrong waivable item

questionsToAsk: Do you have any AOs in a grade below SNCO or GS-7?
Are any LDTAs or ODTAs in your routing lists?
Show me the waiver letters for these personnel?
Who approved the waivers - what is their rank/grade?
verification: Waivable Item: □ AO Grade □ LDTA/ODTA in Routing

0205.title: AO COL TRAINING

regulatory.summary: All Authorizing Officials (AOs) must complete Certifying Officers Legislation (COL) training BEFORE appointment. Annual COL refresher training must be completed by 31 December each year. Training certificates must be retained for review. Inspector Note: Annual refresher training must be completed by the end of the calendar year. Certificates must be provided for review.

actions: Identify all current AOs from DD Form 577 file
Create list: AO names, appointment dates
howToInspect.callout: No AO roster maintained

actions: Ask: "Show me COL training certificates for all AOs."
Should have: Initial COL + annual refreshers for each AO
howToInspect.callout: AO COL certificate missing from file

actions: Compare: Training date to DD Form 577 appointment date
Training must be: BEFORE or same day as appointment
howToInspect.callout: AO appointed without initial COL training

actions: First refresher due: 31 December of same year (if appointed before December)
OR: 31 December of next year (if appointed in December)
How many full calendar years since initial training?
howToInspect.callout: AO refresher training overdue

actions: For each AO: Has current year refresher been completed?
howToInspect.callout: If past 31 December and no refresher

actions: Course title: "Certifying Officers Legislation" or "COL Training"
howToInspect.callout: Certificate details (date, completing official, course version) incomplete

actions: Any AOs appointed in last 6 months?
For each: Initial COL completed before appointment?
If no: FINDING (cannot serve without initial COL)
howToInspect.callout: New AO without training within required window

actions: Any AOs past 31 December without current year refresher?
Action: AO should not certify vouchers until refresher complete
howToInspect.callout: AO with expired training still active in DTS

actions: Filed with DD Form 577 or in training file?
howToInspect.callout: Certificates not retained for current plus previous calendar year

actions: Ask random AO: "When did you complete your last COL training?"
Ask: "When is your next refresher due?"
AO should know: Training status and requirements
howToInspect.callout: AO unable to demonstrate working knowledge of certifying responsibilities

actions: Annual refresher not completed by 31 December

questionsToAsk: Show me COL training certificates for all your AOs?
When were your AOs appointed, and when did they complete initial COL?
Have all AOs completed this year's refresher training?
When is the deadline for annual refresher?
verification: AO roster current and on file.
COL certificate on file for each AO.
Initial COL certificate predates each AO appointment date.
Annual refresher certificate within 12 months for each AO.
AO with expired training has had DTS permissions revoked.
Certificates retained for current plus previous calendar year.

0206.title: APPOINTEE TRAINING

regulatory.summary: ODTAs must receive and retain all required training certificates within two weeks of personnel appointment. Defense Administration Office (DAO) roles require annual refresher by 31 December. Travelers and NDEAs require training every three years. Non-compliant personnel must have permissions removed by LDTA. Inspector Note: DAO annual refresher training must be completed by the end of the calendar year. Notify LDTA to remove permission/accesses for non-compliant personnel. Traveler and NDEA training must be completed every three years. Training certificates must be available for review. This question does not encompass AO initial and refresher COL training.

howToInspect.callout: Appointee training requirement not tracked

actions: Ask: "Show me training certificates for all appointed personnel."
Should be organized: By person, by role, or by date
For each appointee in past year:
howToInspect.callout: If more than 14 days

actions: For each DAO role (ODTA, FDTA, BDTA, RO, DMM):
howToInspect.callout: If past 31 December without refresher
If expired
If expired

actions: Ask: "Have you had any personnel with expired training?"
If yes: "What action did you take?"
Should have: Notified LDTA to remove permissions
Request proof: Email to LDTA, removal documentation
howToInspect.callout: If no action taken

actions: Ask: "What is your process when training expires?"
Should have: Immediate notification to LDTA
Should document: Notification date, removal date
DAO roles: All current year refreshers complete?
Three-year training: Any expiring in next 30 days?
If expirations pending: How is ODTA tracking?
Ask: "How do you track when training is due?"
Should have: Spreadsheet, calendar, automated system
Training certificates not received within two weeks
DAO annual refresher not completed by 31 December
No notification to LDTA for non-compliant personnel
Permissions not removed for expired training
No tracking system for training due dates

questionsToAsk: Show me your training tracking system?
Have all DAO personnel completed this year's refresher?
When did you last notify LDTA about expired training?

verification: List of all appointees requiring training on file.
Appointee training documented within two weeks of appointment.
Three-year refresher training tracked with due dates.
Training rosters retained for current plus previous calendar year.
DTS permissions not assigned before training complete.
DTS permissions revoked immediately on training lapse.

0207.title: CHECK-IN/CHECK-OUT PROCEDURES

regulatory.summary: Commands must have written check-in/check-out procedures ensuring all personnel check in and out with ODTA. Check-in requires creation or receipt of DTS profiles into proper organization. Check-out requires verification of no outstanding advances, unsettled documents, or debt. ODTAs must reconcile personnel rosters against DTS profiles monthly and annotate discrepancies. Inspector Note: The ODTA must create or receive DTS profiles into the designated organization(s). Verify command personnel rosters such as alpha rosters, HR civilian rosters, morning reports, inbound rosters, etc. against the DTS profiles. Annotate the differences and any corrective action required. The ODTA must verify there are no outstanding travel advances, unsettled authorizations, vouchers, or debt in DTS prior to transfer/separation.

actions: Ask: "Show me your DTS check-in/check-out procedures."
Must be: Written (SOP, checklist, or command policy)
howToInspect.callout: No written check-in/check-out procedures

actions: Personnel reports to ODTA during check-in
ODTA creates new DTS profile OR receives profile from losing command
Profile information verified (name, SSN, rank, contact)
Personnel reports to ODTA during check-out
ODTA checks for outstanding travel advances
ODTA checks for outstanding travel debt
All outstanding items resolved before departure
Profile transferred to gaining command OR inactivated
Request: List of personnel who checked in within last 90 days
howToInspect.callout: Sampled check-in did not complete DTS profile

actions: Request: List of personnel who checked out/separated within last 90 days
howToInspect.callout: Sampled check-out did not complete required DTS steps

actions: Request: Current personnel roster (alpha roster, morning report, etc.)
Request: Current DTS profile listing from system
Compare: Personnel roster to DTS profiles
Should be annotated: Differences identified, corrective action noted
howToInspect.callout: Personnel roster does not reconcile to DTS

actions: Personnel on roster but no DTS profile (didn't check in)
DTS profile but not on roster (didn't check out/profile not transferred)
Profile information doesn't match roster (name change, rank change)
howToInspect.callout: Roster discrepancies not addressed

actions: Ask: "When did you last reconcile rosters against DTS?"
Request proof: Annotated roster or comparison document
howToInspect.callout: Monthly reconciliation not documented

howToInspect.step: Check for Outstanding Items at Separation
actions: Ask: "Have you had anyone separate with outstanding travel issues?"
Should have: Resolution before departure OR debt notification to finance
howToInspect.callout: Outstanding items at separation not addressed

actions: Ask ODTA: "Show me how you check for outstanding advances."
Observe: Can ODTA navigate to appropriate reports?
Verify: ODTA knows how to identify outstanding items
Ask: "Is DTS check-in part of your command's overall check-in process?"
Should be: Integrated into admin check-in checklist
Check: S-1/admin knows to send personnel to ODTA
howToInspect.callout: Check-in/check-out not integrated with command process

actions: Personnel not checking in with ODTA (profiles not created)
Personnel not checking out with ODTA
Outstanding advances/vouchers/debt not cleared before departure
Personnel roster not reconciled against DTS profiles
Check-in/out not integrated into command process

questionsToAsk: Show me your written check-in/check-out procedures?
Walk me through what happens when someone checks into your command?
What do you do if someone is checking out with an outstanding voucher?
When did you last reconcile your personnel roster against DTS profiles?

0208.title: UNSUBMITTED VOUCHER REPORT

regulatory.summary: ODTAs must extract and reconcile the Unsubmitted Voucher Report weekly, notify travelers, AOs, and APCs of unsubmitted vouchers beyond the five-business-day requirement, and retain reports for current plus previous calendar year. Inspector Note: The ODTA must provide evidence of notifying the AO and APC of outstanding travel vouchers to assist with timely settlement.

actions: Ask: "Show me your Unsubmitted Voucher Reports."
Should have: Weekly reports for current year + all of previous year
Minimum reports: 52 weeks per year 104+ reports
howToInspect.callout: No Unsubmitted Voucher Reports on file

actions: Look for: Consistent day of week (e.g., every Monday)
howToInspect.callout: Reports not extracted weekly

actions: Example: From the current inspection month, calculate back 24 months
howToInspect.callout: Reports not retained current plus previous calendar year

actions: Reports must be reconciled (not just extracted)
Look for: Annotations, notes, action taken
Vouchers identified as overdue (5+ business days)
Travelers must submit vouchers within 5 business days of return
Calculate: Return date to current date
howToInspect.callout: Vouchers exceeding 5 business days not identified

actions: Evidence: Email, DTS message, phone log
howToInspect.callout: Traveler notification not documented for overdue voucher

actions: Purpose: AO can direct traveler to submit
howToInspect.callout: AO notification not documented for overdue voucher

actions: Purpose: Coordinate GTCC payment with voucher settlement
howToInspect.callout: APC notification not documented for overdue voucher

actions: Select 3-5 overdue vouchers from recent report

howToInspect.callout: Notification documentation incomplete

actions: Ask: "What do you do if traveler doesn't submit after notification?"
May include: Supervisor involvement, command discipline
Check: Process is documented and followed
howToInspect.callout: No documented follow-up process

actions: Point to unsubmitted voucher: "When did this person return?"
howToInspect.callout: ODTA unable to demonstrate working knowledge of unsubmitted voucher procedure

actions: Reports not retained for required period

questionsToAsk: Show me your Unsubmitted Voucher Reports for the past year?
How often do you pull this report?
Show me examples of notifications you sent for overdue vouchers?
Who do you notify when vouchers aren't submitted on time?

0209.title: APPROVED STATUS REPORT (FDTA)

regulatory.summary: FDTAs must extract the Approved Status Report every two weeks, reconcile all rejected vouchers with documented actions taken, retain system header data unaltered, maintain correspondence and notification records, and retain reports for current plus previous calendar year. Inspector Note: Reconciliation includes verifying all rejects, report annotations, actions taken to resolve rejects, correspondence, and notification records. Do not remove system header data from the report. The FDTA must demonstrate the validation process.

actions: Verify: FDTA appointed via DD Form 577
Note: This inspection conducted with FDTA, not ODTA
howToInspect.callout: No FDTA identified or appointed

actions: Ask FDTA: "Show me your Approved Status Reports."
Should have: Bi-weekly (every two weeks) for current + previous year
howToInspect.callout: Approved Status Reports not on file

actions: Check: Reports dated every two weeks
Count days between reports: Should be approximately 14 days
howToInspect.callout: Bi-weekly extraction cadence not met

actions: Current year: All bi-weekly reports present?
Previous year: All bi-weekly reports present?
howToInspect.callout: Retention period not met

actions: CRITICAL: System header must NOT be altered
Check: Report title, date, system info intact
Look for: Deleted columns, modified data, reformatted
howToInspect.callout: Report header integrity not validated

actions: Review reports: Are there any rejects?
howToInspect.callout: AR REJECT vouchers not identified
Rejects not reconciled or addressed

actions: Look for: Handwritten notes, highlights, comments
howToInspect.callout: Report annotations incomplete

howToInspect.step: Verify Actions Taken to Resolve Rejects
howToInspect.callout: No documented action to resolve rejects

actions: For rejects: Should have correspondence file
howToInspect.callout: Correspondence records missing for AO contact

actions: FDTA must notify ODTA of rejects
Check: Notification log or email proof
Timing: How quickly after reject identified?
Should be: Immediate (same day or next business day)
howToInspect.callout: Notification records missing

actions: Ask FDTA: "Walk me through how you reconcile this report."
howToInspect.callout: Validation process not demonstrable
Sampled recent rejects not properly resolved

questionsToAsk: Show me your Approved Status Reports for the past year?
How often do you extract this report?
Walk me through how you reconcile rejected vouchers?
Show me correspondence and notifications for recent rejects?

0210.title: TRAVELER VOUCHER SUBMISSION

regulatory.summary: Travelers must create and sign their travel vouchers within five business days of returning from travel. Submission is complete when the traveler signs the voucher, not when it is approved or paid. Inspector Note: The voucher must be created and signed within the specified period.

howToInspect.step: Select Sample of Recent Completed Travel
actions: Timeframe: Travel completed within last 90 days
10 travelers minimum for small commands (less than 50 travelers)
20 travelers minimum for medium commands (51-200 travelers)
30 travelers minimum for large commands (200+ travelers)
Find: Voucher submission date (traveler signature date)
Check authorization: End date of travel
Use whichever is actual return date
Important: Count business days only (exclude weekends and holidays)
Voucher submission date traveler SIGNED voucher
Check: Date stamp on traveler signature block
Start: Day after return from travel
Due: By end of business Monday, 8 March
howToInspect.callout: Traveler voucher submitted more than 5 business days after return
0-5 business days
Acceptable error rate exceeded across sample
No corrective action on late submissions

actions: Ask: "How do you ensure travelers submit vouchers on time?"
howToInspect.callout: No command emphasis on voucher submission timeliness

actions: Ask traveler: "Do you know the 5-day requirement?"
howToInspect.callout: Voucher creation process not aligned with traveler responsibilities

actions: Suggests: Training need, policy emphasis, or process improvement
howToInspect.callout: Systemic late submission issues identified but unresolved

actions: Vouchers submitted 6+ business days after return
No command emphasis on timely submission

questionsToAsk: Do your travelers know they have 5 business days to submit vouchers?
How do you monitor voucher submission timeliness?
Show me recent travel and the voucher submission dates?
What do you do when travelers consistently submit late?
verification: Status: □ On Time (0-5 days) □ Late (6+ days)

0211.title: VOUCHER ROUTING TIMELINE

regulatory.summary: After a traveler signs a voucher, it must be routed through AO/RO approval to the servicing finance office within three business days. Exception: FSTS Office vouchers exempt. Route Status Report should be used to monitor compliance. Inspector Note: The only exceptions are those vouchers routed to Formal School Training Support (FSTS) Office. Commands should use the Route Status Report as a tool to monitor routing and documents pending action over three business days.

howToInspect.step: Select Sample of Recently Signed Vouchers
actions: Timeframe: Vouchers signed within last 60 days
Include: Vouchers at various routing stages
Identify: Date voucher arrived at finance office
Check voucher: Date stamp on traveler signature
Day 1 starts: Next business day after signature
Finance arrival date finance received for approval
May appear as: "FDTA received" or "Finance pending"
Due at finance: By end of business Thursday, 4 March
howToInspect.callout: Voucher to finance office exceeds 3 business days

actions: Ask: "Is this voucher for school training?"
If routed to FSTS Office: Exempt from 3-day rule
FSTS vouchers: Mark as EXEMPT (not counted)
howToInspect.callout: 0-3 business days
Acceptable error rate exceeded across sample
No corrective action on late routing

actions: Ask: "Show me your Route Status Report."
Report shows: Documents pending at each routing stage
Look for: Vouchers pending over 3 days
Ask: "How often do you pull this report?"
howToInspect.callout: Route Status Report not reviewed
Routing delays identified but unaddressed

actions: Ask: "Show me a typical voucher routing list."
Should be: Traveler → RO (if applicable) → AO → Finance
howToInspect.callout: Routing list configuration issues unresolved

actions: AO on leave with no alternate
howToInspect.callout: Routing bottlenecks not identified or addressed

actions: Ask: "How do you monitor the 3-day requirement?"
Should have: Process to follow up on delays
howToInspect.callout: No command monitoring of voucher routing

actions: Vouchers taking 4+ business days to reach finance

questionsToAsk: Show me the Route Status Report?
How do you monitor the 3-day routing requirement?
What causes routing delays in your command?
Show me the routing history for this voucher?
verification: Status: □ On Time (0-3 days) □ Late (4+ days) □ Exempt (FSTS)
